title: US PPI Forecast Snapshot — July 2026 (Composite Calibrated Bridge Nowcast v2.5 Enhanced)
summary: MEASURED MONTH: JULY 2026 (released Aug 13, 2026 — NOT the June print due Jul 15). July PPI forecast: Headline +0.4% MoM, Core +0.3% MoM, provisional YoY ~6.0%/5.0%. This is a mid-month nowcast: July is ~45% complete, so all component inputs are provisional and confidence is cut to 50. The July story is the mirror image of June: the US-Iran re-escalation over the Jul 11-13 weekend sent WTI +9% to $77.85 and Brent to $79.11, with Iran claiming the Strait of Hormuz shut (6 vessels Sunday, fewest in 5 weeks). AAA regular gasoline reversed from a month of declines: $3.797 -> $3.872 WoW (+2.0%), diesel $4.756 -> $4.875 (+2.5%). EIA w/e Jul 3 shows the fuel-supply crunch deepening — distillate -5.0M to 103.6M (12% below 5-yr avg) even as crude builds +3.0M. Offset: Henry Hub Aug contract $2.89 (-8% MoM) caps the energy composite. ISM Prices Paid 73.0 (June print, July ISM due Aug 3) keeps core goods hot; ECI Q1 +0.9% SA (+0.30 monthly proxy, Q2 due Jul 31) anchors services. Base effect matters: July 2025 PPI was +0.7% MoM, so even a +0.4% July print pulls YoY down toward ~6.0%.
baseCase: July 2026 PPI: Headline +0.4% MoM, Core +0.3% MoM. War premium in crude (+12% Brent month-to-date) and the distillate crunch lift July energy composite to +2.7% (provisional, half-month). Core goods +0.3% on ISM 73 passthrough; services +0.23% via ECI. Intermediate-demand uplift +0.15 ppt. YoY drifts to ~6.0% on the +0.7% July-2025 base effect.

release.name: PPI
release.thesis: July's measured month flips the energy sign: the US-Iran re-escalation (WTI +9% to $77.85, Brent $79.11, Hormuz transit at 5-week low) plus a deepening distillate crunch (12% below 5-yr avg) push July producer energy costs back up after June's collapse. ISM Prices Paid 73.0 and ECI-anchored services keep core sticky near +0.3%. Base effect (+0.7% in July 2025) pulls YoY toward ~6.0% even with a firm MoM. Mid-month nowcast: half of July unsampled — a ceasefire could erase the energy contribution entirely.

scenarios.label: Base Case — War Premium Persists Partially
scenarios.outcome: July headline PPI +0.4% MoM, core +0.3% MoM, YoY ~6.0%/5.0%. Energy composite +2.7% on war premium + distillate crunch, partially capped by weak Henry Hub ($2.89). Core sticky via ISM 73 / ECI.
scenarios.marketReadthrough: Stagflation bid builds: energy + gold outperform, long duration capped. YoY dipping under 6% on base effects gives doves a talking point, but MoM re-acceleration keeps the Fed hawkish.
scenarios.invalidation: Ceasefire re-signed before mid-July sampling; WTI back under $70 for two consecutive weeks

scenarios.label: Hot Case — Sustained Hormuz Disruption
scenarios.outcome: July headline PPI +0.8% MoM, core +0.5% MoM. Strait transit stays throttled through the July sampling window; Brent holds $80+; diesel/jet crack spreads widen on the inventory crunch; energy composite +5-6%.
scenarios.marketReadthrough: Full stagflation repricing: 2Y Treasury jumps, hike odds for 2026 rise above 70%, USD firms, equities de-rate. July CPI (Aug 12) one day earlier amplifies the move.
scenarios.invalidation: Diplomatic resolution + OPEC+ supply response; EIA shows product inventories normalizing

scenarios.label: Cool Case — Ceasefire + Mean Reversion
scenarios.outcome: July headline PPI 0.0% MoM, core +0.1% MoM. Ceasefire re-signed, crude mean-reverts to $70, war premium evaporates before mid-month sampling; natgas weakness (-8% MoM) adds drag; core goods cool with DXY ~101.
scenarios.marketReadthrough: Inflation scare unwinds: rate-hike pricing fades, duration rallies, growth equities recover. YoY slides toward 5.7-5.8% on the base effect.
scenarios.invalidation: Any kinetic escalation during the sampling window; distillate crunch triggers diesel spike regardless of crude

setups.bias: Long into the July sampling window while Hormuz risk is live
setups.trigger: Strait transit stays below 10 vessels/day; EIA distillate stocks stay 12%+ below 5-yr avg; WTI > $75
setups.invalidation: Signed ceasefire + WTI weekly close below $70

setups.bias: Long as stagflation/geopolitical hedge through the CPI+PPI double-header (Aug 12-13)
setups.trigger: Hot July CPI/PPI stack with war escalation; real yields capped by growth scare
setups.invalidation: Cool prints + ceasefire: real yields pop, gold de-rates below key support

setups.bias: Short duration on hot-case confirmation; long only on cool stack
setups.trigger: July core PPI > +0.4% with hot CPI — sell duration
setups.invalidation: Cool CPI+PPI stack flips to recession-pricing — cover and go long

setups.bias: Long on hot July prints with rates follow-through
setups.trigger: Headline + core both firm; terminal-rate repricing lifts; DXY breaks 102
setups.invalidation: Cool prints + ceasefire; DXY back under 100

watchItems.label: MEASURED MONTH = JULY 2026
watchItems.value: Release Aug 13, 2026 (08:30 ET) — verify vs BLS schedule
watchItems.note: This file forecasts July price changes. The June PPI (Jul 15) is a separate, earlier release and only enters here as the prior-month anchor.

watchItems.label: PROVISIONAL — July sampling incomplete
watchItems.note: All composite inputs are July-to-date through Jul 13 close. Second-half July data (esp. energy, post any ceasefire) can swing the energy composite by ±3 ppt.

watchItems.label: US-Iran / Hormuz
watchItems.note: Dominant July input. Trump says shipping open; Iran claims strait shut. Resolution timing decides base vs hot case.

watchItems.label: EIA fuel-supply crunch
watchItems.note: Products tight regardless of crude direction. Russia diesel export ban + China export uncertainty widen the crude-products divide into July.

watchItems.label: June PPI actual (Jul 15, 08:30 ET)
watchItems.note: Becomes the prior-month anchor for the July YoY math once published. Revisions to May possible.

watchItems.label: BLS ECI Q2 2026 (Jul 31)
watchItems.note: Lands inside the July measurement window — Q2 >1.0% lifts the July services composite.

watchItems.label: ISM Manufacturing (Jul data, ~Aug 1-3)
watchItems.note: July ISM Prices Paid is the last major core-goods input; >70 keeps the hot case alive.

watchItems.label: VIX regime boundary
watchItems.note: Close above 18 flips model to Elevated regime (2-month bias window, wider CI).

keyDrivers: July energy sign flip: after June's -23% Brent collapse, July-to-date crude is +12% (Brent $71.4 -> $80.1) on the US-Iran re-escalation. Retail gasoline reversed: AAA $3.797 -> $3.872 WoW; diesel $4.756 -> $4.875. Energy composite provisional at +2.7%.

Distillate crunch is structural, not geopolitical: stocks 12% below the 5-yr average at 103.6M bbl with Russia's diesel export ban and refinery-capacity losses — diesel/jet stay bid even if crude cools, supporting July PPI energy either way.
Core stickiness unchanged: ISM Prices Paid 73.0 (+2.3 ppt normalized) plus ECI Q1 +0.9% SA keep July core near +0.3% MoM. July ISM (Aug 1-3) and ECI Q2 (Jul 31) are the two remaining core inputs.
Base effect cuts YoY: July 2025 PPI was +0.7% MoM, so a +0.4% July print mechanically pulls headline YoY from ~6.2-6.5% toward ~6.0% — MoM re-acceleration hidden inside a softer YoY.
Natural gas is the offset: Henry Hub Aug $2.89 (-8% MoM) despite the heat dome, keeping the energy composite from running hotter.
risks: Mid-month provisional risk: ~55% of the July measurement window is unsampled. A ceasefire in the next two weeks converts the base case into the cool case (headline 0.0%).
Upside — sustained disruption: if Hormuz transit stays throttled into early August, the hot case (+0.8% headline) becomes the base; diesel cracks amplify it via the inventory crunch.

Downside — sampling-window timing: July PPI samples the week of Jul 13 (Tue Jul 14) — prices collected this week may already embed the spike, meaning a later-July ceasefire would NOT fully reverse the print.
Data risk: June PPI (Jul 15) and its May revisions reset the prior-month anchor used in July YoY math; a big June surprise shifts the July YoY estimate by up to ±0.3 ppt.
Release-date risk: Aug 13, 2026 is inferred from the BLS 1-day-after-CPI pattern (July CPI = Aug 12); verify against the official BLS schedule when published.
sources: https://gasprices.aaa.com/ (AAA regular $3.872, diesel $4.875, Jul 13 2026; week ago $3.797/$4.756)
https://crudesignal.io/crude-oil-inventory-report (EIA w/e Jul 3: crude +3.0M to 411.4M, distillate -5.0M to 103.6M, SPR -6.2M)
https://tanktransport.com/2026/07/fuel-supply-crunch/ (EIA WPSR Jul 8: distillate 12% below 5-yr avg, utilization 95.8%)
https://www.textileworld.com/textile-world/2026/07/manufacturing-pmi-at-53-3-june-2026 (ISM PMI 53.3, Prices Paid 73.0, Jun 2026 — proxy for July)
https://www.usinflationcalculator.com/inflation/consumer-price-index-release-schedule/ (BLS 2026 CPI schedule: Jul 2026 CPI = Aug 12, 2026; PPI follows 1 day after CPI pattern)
